description: AIMS of the bolshevik GOVERNMENT IN RUSSIA It Is Not Experimenting with Political and Economic Institu tions Correspondent Says but Is Building a Society Which Labor Will Create and Direct By ALEXANDER TRACHTENBERG DlreNtor Department of Labor Research Rand School of Social Science To the Editor of the Evening Post The metropolitan press your esteemed journal in particular have recently been devoting a great deal of space to Russian affairs The Soviet government has come in for a great deal of criticism from its enemies both in the bourgeois and Socialist camps The first are hurling anathema at the present Russian government because they see in it a challenge to the existing social and economic system The others although not a very numerous group are doing ail within their power to discredit the Bolshevik government beCause they bear a traditional hatred toward the Bolsheviks There are those who cal themselves Socialists and who would rather see the Czar restored to his throne than have Russia under the control of the Bolsheviks While in Russia the writer belonged to the socalled Menshevik wing of the Social Democratic party He still is in disagreement with the Bolsheviks on a number of tactical
